name: Patient Records & Consent (GMC / NHS aligned)
description: Reference policy aligned with GMC Good medical practice and the NHS Records Management Code of Practice.

data.text: Patient records & consent — GMC / NHS aligned
Clinical notes are clear, accurate, contemporaneous, and detailed enough for another doctor to continue care.
GP records are retained at least 10 years after the last entry; longer retention applies for some scenarios (e.g. children, mental health).
Informed consent is documented (Montgomery-style) — risks, benefits, reasonable alternatives, and the patient's decision — for procedures and significant treatment changes.
Prescribing records identify medicine, dose, frequency, duration, indication, and any controlled-drug requirements.
PHI processing complies with UK GDPR, the Data Protection Act 2018, and the common-law duty of confidentiality; lawful bases for sharing are recorded.
Handover and follow-up plans are recorded clearly enough for the receiving clinician.

clauses.title: Notes sufficient for handover
clauses.body: Clinical notes must be clear, accurate, contemporaneous, and detailed enough for another doctor to continue care.

clauses.title: 10-year GP retention
clauses.body: GP records must be retained at least 10 years after last entry; longer for children and mental-health records.
clauses.source_citation: NHS Records Management Code of Practice

clauses.title: Montgomery-style consent
clauses.body: Informed consent must be documented covering risks, benefits, reasonable alternatives, and the patient's decision for procedures and significant treatment changes.

clauses.title: Prescribing records complete
clauses.body: Prescribing records must identify medicine, dose, frequency, duration, indication, and any controlled-drug requirements.

clauses.title: Lawful basis for PHI sharing
clauses.body: PHI processing must comply with UK GDPR, the Data Protection Act 2018, and the common-law duty of confidentiality; lawful bases for sharing must be recorded.

clauses.title: Continuity of care documented
clauses.body: Handover and follow-up plans must be recorded clearly enough for the receiving clinician.
